feedback.text: Student of the Jean Monnet Modules project “DERECHO”: ‘Defense of European Rights and Excellence for Consumers HHarmonisation’, No 101239437. Project financed by the European Union. Jean Monnet Actions in the field of Higher Education: Modules. Call for tenders ERASMUS-JMO-2025-HEI-TCH-RSCH. As a student of this project, I have been able to examine this directive in depth, and therefore I would like to give my opinion on it. First of all, I consider it quite important that we take into account that technologies and society are developing and therefore today’s realities are not the same as those of a few years ago. For this reason, I believe it is essential that there should be more regulation with regard to the issue of minors, since they are vulnerable to these advances and are influenced not only by the so-called ‘false reality’ of social media as instagram, but also by the following figures such as influencers. In this regard, due to the low demand that exists in applications to identify themselves, minors often follow content that ultimately affects their mental health and development. Therefore, there should be more stringent regulation for the identification of minors in apps and also measures to try to reduce the negative messages of influencers. In this second idea, we must bear in mind that, as they are public figures, their ideas are either good or bad, reaching many parts of society, and many vulnerable subjects. Secondly, I would like to mention the problem of acquiring ownership of digital products, as despite the fact that the concept of acquiring ownership of a product should mean having complete disposition of it, today in the digital world it does not mean the same. More so in the case of video games, it is no longer only that you do not have complete disposition of a game you bought, but they can also take you out of the game, removing the right to use it from buyers, even when they have paid for it. As a final idea, I would like to talk about the case of the terms and conditions of the websites. Personally, I never read the conditions I am marking because it is an issue that appears on every page on which I am in, and that is why I think there is really little to do for us to read them. That is why I believe that it is now well regulated and would not change anything else. In conclusion, I consider that this new law should be adapted to the new social reality in which we live, where many more people have access to the digital world, in a much easier and more common way.
